title: Show failure state in refresh timestamps and add JS robustness guards
analysis:
The refresh indicator JS in base.html (lines 45-73) has four robustness gaps:

1. **Stale timestamp on error**: The error-card IIFE (lines 34-43) shows a red card on htmx:responseError/timeout/sendError, but the timestamp tracking IIFE (lines 45-73) only updates timestamps on htmx:afterSwap (success). On error, the timestamp keeps showing stale time (e.g. 'Updated 5m ago') with no indication that refreshes are failing. Fix: also listen on error events in the timestamp IIFE and set stamp text to 'Update failed — retrying…', plus mark a data-attribute so the setInterval doesn't overwrite the failure message.

2. **Missing NaN guard**: Line 62 does parseInt(el.getAttribute('data-updated-at'), 10) which returns NaN if the attribute is empty or malformed. The elapsed calculation then produces NaN, leading to 'Updated NaNm ago'. Fix: add isNaN(ts) early return.

3. **animationend may not fire**: Lines 54-57 add section-refreshed class and wait for animationend to remove it. With prefers-reduced-motion, the browser may skip the animation entirely, so animationend never fires, leaving the class (and its border-radius) applied permanently, plus leaking the listener reference. Fix: add a setTimeout(700ms) fallback that removes the class if animationend hasn't fired.

4. **setInterval not stored**: Line 59's setInterval return value is discarded. While not a bug today, storing it enables future cleanup. Fix: assign to a named variable.

Existing test pattern: HTML string inspection via TestClient. Tests check for presence of JS keywords, event listener names, CSS classes. We follow this same pattern — tests verify the presence of the fix's characteristic code in the rendered HTML.

steps.description: Write failing tests for error-state timestamp updates. Add TestTimestampErrorState class to test_refresh_indicator.py with tests that: (a) the JS contains a function that handles error events and references data-updated-for stamps, (b) 'Update failed' text appears in the JS, (c) a data-update-failed attribute is set to prevent the interval from overwriting the failure message. All three should fail against the current base.html.

Implement error-state timestamp updates in base.html. In the timestamp-tracking IIFE, add a function that: (1) extracts data-section from evt.detail.target, (2) finds the matching data-updated-for element, (3) sets its textContent to 'Update failed \u2014 retrying\u2026', (4) sets a data-update-failed attribute. Register this function on htmx:responseError, htmx:timeout, htmx:sendError. In the htmx:afterSwap handler, clear the data-update-failed attribute on recovery. In the setInterval, skip elements that have data-update-failed set.

Write failing test for NaN guard. Add TestNaNGuard class to test_refresh_indicator.py with a test that the JS contains 'isNaN' in the context of the setInterval timestamp updater. Should fail against current base.html.
Add NaN guard in base.html. After the parseInt call on line 62, add: if (isNaN(ts)) return; — this prevents 'Updated NaNm ago' when data-updated-at is empty or malformed.

Write failing test for animation cleanup fallback. Add TestAnimationFallback class to test_refresh_indicator.py with a test that the JS contains a setTimeout fallback near section-refreshed class removal, ensuring the class gets removed even when animationend doesn't fire (prefers-reduced-motion). Should fail against current base.html.

Add animation cleanup fallback in base.html. After adding the section-refreshed class and the animationend listener, add a setTimeout(700ms) that removes the class if it's still present. Use a flag variable or check classList.contains to avoid double-removal. The 700ms > 600ms animation duration provides a safe margin.

Write failing test for stored setInterval reference. Add TestIntervalCleanup class to test_refresh_indicator.py with a test that the JS assigns the setInterval return value to a variable (pattern: 'var <name> = setInterval' or similar). Should fail against current base.html where setInterval is called without storing the result.
Store setInterval reference in base.html. Change bare 'setInterval(function...' to 'var refreshTimer = setInterval(function...' to store the interval ID for potential future cleanup.

design_decisions.decision: Use data-update-failed attribute to prevent setInterval from overwriting failure message
design_decisions.rationale: The setInterval runs every 1s and would immediately overwrite 'Update failed — retrying…' with a stale timestamp. A data attribute flag lets the interval skip elements in failure state. The htmx:afterSwap handler clears this flag on recovery, restoring normal timestamp updates.

design_decisions.decision: Use setTimeout(700ms) fallback rather than matchMedia check for animation cleanup
design_decisions.rationale: A setTimeout fallback is more robust than checking matchMedia because: (a) it handles any case where animationend doesn't fire (not just prefers-reduced-motion), (b) the 700ms > 600ms animation duration is a safe margin, (c) if animationend fires first, the setTimeout just does a no-op classList.remove. A matchMedia-only approach would miss other edge cases like display:none during animation.

design_decisions.decision: Extract error-stamp-update into a named function shared between all three error event listeners
design_decisions.rationale: Same pattern as the existing showError function in the error-card IIFE. Avoids code duplication across htmx:responseError, htmx:timeout, and htmx:sendError listeners.

design_decisions.decision: Follow existing HTML string-inspection test pattern rather than adding a JS test runner
design_decisions.rationale: The project tests JS behavior by inspecting rendered HTML for characteristic strings. Adding a proper JS testing framework (jsdom, playwright) would be a scope expansion beyond this task. The string-inspection tests verify the fix's code is present in the rendered page.

reuse.how: The existing error-card IIFE defines a showError function and registers it on three htmx error events. The timestamp error handler follows the same pattern: define a named function, register on the same three events.
The error handler reuses the same DOM lookup pattern: get data-section from evt.detail.target, then querySelector for matching data-updated-for element.
All new tests follow the existing pattern: client.get('/').text then assert presence of characteristic strings in the rendered HTML.
All new test classes use the existing 'client' fixture — no new fixtures needed.
